_comment: Copy to datasets.local.json (gitignored). Keep RAW files under data/private/datasets/<dataset_id>/ or point roots at your own local mirrors. Public docs should use dataset IDs, not absolute machine paths.

clrs589_project_camera.description: Fujifilm X-T100 ColorChecker-SG / dark / flat / sphere / validation captures
clrs589_project_camera.timeline_note: Capture and reference are different course/project timelines; use project provenance, not camera EXIF dates.
clrs589_project_camera._reference_note: Local CLRS-589 spectroradiometry is neutral-only, but a compatible 2019 ColorChecker-SG spectral-reflectance workbook can be used for the colored CCM/deltaE demo when copied locally. Keep all private/reference files under data/private/references/ and document whether each reference is official nominal, compatible standard, representative measured, or exact per-unit.
clrs589_project_camera.sg_chart_edition: after_nov2014 | before_nov2014 (set to match the physical chart)

spectral_sensitivity_2016_2017.description: 2016/2017 camera spectral-sensitivity monochromator/camSPECS sweep archive mirror
spectral_sensitivity_2016_2017.timeline_note: Use project/archive provenance, not camera EXIF dates. This dataset is camera spectral-sensitivity sweep data, not ColorChecker-SG chart reflectance.
spectral_sensitivity_2016_2017._provenance_note: Captures are Fernando's archive captures; the original 2017 DPMI analysis was a team effort. Treat spectral_v2_1.py as legacy Gold legacy method context and reimplement fresh in C++.
spectral_sensitivity_2016_2017._copy_policy: Do not bulk-copy the full archive by default. Keep the archive read-only and copy only the scoped camera subset, dark frames, SPD sidecars, and metadata needed for the current slice.
spectral_sensitivity_2016_2017._reference_note: Do not confuse this camera-sensitivity dataset with data/private/references/sg_2016_archive/monochromator_color_checker, which is SG chart reflectance reference data.

canon_5d2_repro.description: Canon EOS 5D Mark II reproduction / white-frame validation subset
